summary: Late‑era 1994 show shines with a soaring “So Many Roads” and a stellar “Althea,” captured on a soundboard that many find a bit dry but still listenable.
review: Fans rave about the emotional power of Jerry’s vocal on “So Many Roads” and the jam‑filled “Althea,” calling this one of the finest late‑’94 performances. The recording is listed as a soundboard source, though several listeners note it sounds parched or dry, giving the audio a fair rather than excellent rating.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈70%) are 4‑5 stars praising the performance, while a few note audio quality issues; weighted average yields ~4.0.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No explicit technical details beyond the .sbd label; several reviewers describe the sound as parched, dry and somewhat brittle, while others note no major issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Listeners highlight a strong emotional arc, especially the heartfelt delivery of “So Many Roads” and an energetic jam on “Althea,” marking this as a standout late‑era show.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for inspired, thoughtful playing and moving vocal performance, especially on “So Many Roads.”
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keyboard) noted as playing very well.
